title: Guide RNA (gRNA)
summary: A guide RNA directs Cas9 to a genomic target through Watson-Crick base pairing between its spacer sequence and the complementary protospacer DNA.

sections.heading: Spacer design
sections.content: Most SpCas9 guide designs use a 20 nucleotide spacer upstream of an NGG PAM. Productive guides balance sufficient target binding with specificity, avoiding extreme GC content, repetitive motifs, and obvious sequence features that reduce performance.

sections.heading: Scoring considerations
sections.content: Guide scoring models commonly incorporate GC content, position-specific nucleotides, self-complementarity, sequence complexity, chromatin accessibility, and empirically trained activity data. Lightweight tools may use transparent heuristics for early triage.

key_points: The spacer sequence determines the primary genomic target.
GC content near 40-65% is often a practical starting range, not a guarantee of activity.
Guide quality should be assessed alongside off-target risk and experimental validation.
limitations: Guide heuristics cannot replace empirical validation. Cell type, locus accessibility, delivery method, and assay design strongly influence observed editing efficiency.
